description_long: This is an ornate, vintage candelabrum with five candle holders arranged in a symmetrical, branching pattern. The central stem is elaborately decorated with floral motifs and raised reliefs, leading to a broad, intricately patterned base that provides stability. Each arm curves outward and upward, ending in a square, flared candle cup with detailed edging and a weathered, patinated finish. The surface shows signs of age, with a mottled gray and silver coloration and areas of tarnish, suggesting it is made of metal, likely cast iron or brass. The overall design is highly decorative, with baroque influences and a sense of historical craftsmanship. This object would typically be found as a decorative amenity in urban parks, plazas, or historical public spaces.
description: An ornate, vintage five-arm candelabrum with detailed floral motifs and a weathered metallic finish. The lamp features a broad, decorated base and square candle holders with intricate edging.
description_view_0: Front view showing three candle holders in the foreground and two in the back, highlighting the central stem and ornate base.
description_view_1: Side view with two candle holders aligned, revealing the curvature of the arms and the detailed relief on the base.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the back arms and the symmetry of the five candle holders, with the central stem partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the branching arms and the square, flared candle cups, with the ornate base visible.
